mission.title: kcp: Epic: reverse tunnel to pclusters through syncer for pod subresources
mission.description: Epic: reverse tunnel to pclusters through syncer for pod subresources. Requested by 1+ users.

steps.title: Check current kcp deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kcp version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp
helm list -n kcp 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kcp installation.

steps.title: Review kcp configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kcp configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp
kubectl get configmap -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kcp
```
The kcp apiserver has to talk to the physical cluster for `kubectl exec|attach|logs` because pods are running there through the syncer.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Epic: reverse tunnel to pclusters through syncer for pod
steps.description:
## Summary

Add functionality to the Syncer to establish reverse connections and act as "reverse proxy for the downstream clusters.

The KCP server exposes 2 URLs that allows to proxy over reverse connections with the following format
-
```yaml
kubectl --server=https://{host}/services/syncer-tunnels/clusters/<ws>/apis/workload.kcp.dev/v1alpha1/synctargets/<name>/proxy get deployments -A
NAMESPACE                      NAME                           READY   UP-TO-DATE   AVAILABLE   AGE
kcp-syncer-cluster1-195k2b7i   kcp-syncer-cluster1-195k2b7i   1/1     1            1           34m
kube-system                    coredns                        2/2     2            2           4h53m
local-path-storage             local-path-provisioner         1/1     1            1           4h53m
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp
kubectl get events -n kcp --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Epic: reverse tunnel to pclusters through syncer for pod" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Summary

Add functionality to the Syncer to establish reverse connections and act as "reverse proxy for the downstream clusters.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kcp installed or the issue environment reproducible.
